exemplar.title: To what extent does age influence the speed of swimming athletes between the ages of 8 to 18 as measured by performance metrics in 50m freestyle sprints?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequence of introduction, data, analysis, evaluation and conclusion
Well-organised with headings, sub-headings, contents table and numbered sections
Logical development of mathematical argument without major jumps
weaknesses: Lengthy narrative and repeated explanations create redundancy
Minor mis-ordering in tables and duplicate headings disrupt flow
Some formatting inconsistencies (inline math in bullets, caption placement)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands at band 3 (coherence and organization) and one at band 2 (conciseness) yield an overall band 3. The exploration is coherent and well structured, but verbosity and minor presentation issues prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Effective use of tables, scatter plots, trend lines and rank plots to support analysis
Mostly consistent mathematical language with clear definitions of key symbols
Good logical flow with explanations and interpretations following each calculation
weaknesses: Notation errors (e.g. misuse of decimal commas, ambiguous expressions like 8.52.84)
Missing axis labels, units and legends on several figures
Omitted intermediate steps reduce clarity in some calculations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands assessed at band 3 and one at band 2, the work demonstrates good mathematical communication overall. Representations are effective and the logic is clear, but notation inaccuracies and incomplete detailing limit it to band 3.

strengths: Authentic motivation from personal swimming experience
Independent data collection and initiative in comparing Pearson and Spearman methods
First-person framing shows clear personal approach
weaknesses: Analytical path remains fairly standard for AA SL
Personal approach, while clear, lacks innovative depth
Creativity is good but not exceptional in method choice
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall in band 2. The investigation clearly reflects the studentâs interest and initiative, but does not reach the outstanding or highly innovative levels required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on sample-size limits and external variables
Thoughtful analysis of correlation coefficients and method comparison
Clear evaluation of limitations and suggestions for future improvement
weaknesses: Reflection stops mid-discussion in places, indicating incomplete exposition
Lacks deeper statistical insight (e.g. significance testing, confidence intervals)
Critical analysis, while present, is not sustained at a high level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each reflection strand is band 2. The student provides meaningful critique and evaluation, but the level of depth and critical insight does not meet the criteria for band 3.

strengths: Appropriate selection and logical sequencing of Pearson, Spearman and regression techniques
Mathematics fully commensurate with course level and applied to student-collected data
Clear link between research aim and mathematical planning
weaknesses: Numerical and notation errors (mis-typed Î£xy, inconsistent intercept values)
Unsupported reference to t-test without calculation
Discrepancies in table data and calculations reduce accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands at band 3 (relevance and level appropriateness) and one at band 2 (accuracy), the overall performance aligns with band 3. The mathematics is relevant and well applied, but errors prevent a higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each calculation, results are interpreted immediately after they appear, and the reader can trace the argument.  Nonetheless, calculation errors and fragmented sentences in places reduce clarity slightly, so the work demonstrates âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ logical communication.

The student shows conceptual understanding of correlation and regression, but numerical errors (mis-typed Î£xy, impossible intercept value in graph, misuse of decimal notation) reveal gaps in accuracy.  Interpretations of r and RÂ² are broadly correct, so understanding is more than âlimitedâ, yet the error rate prevents awarding higher than band 2.

The student employs tables of raw data, calculated means, scatter diagrams, trend lines and rank plots.  These representations match the analytical steps and aid interpretation; however, axis labelling is occasionally sparse and some graphics are low-resolution.  Overall the use is effective but not sophisticated enough for the highest band.

The report follows a clear sequence (introduction â data â analysis â evaluation â conclusion) and the mathematical argument generally progresses without major jumps.  Each analytical technique is introduced, executed and then commented on, so the reader can follow how the investigation develops.  There are occasional digressions and some mis-ordering within tables, but these do not seriously disrupt the overall logical flow; thus the exploration is coherent, though not exemplary.

Designing and timing the sample, producing own graphs, and comparing Pearson with Spearman illustrates creativity and initiative.  Yet the mathematical techniques themselves are routine for AA SL, so creativity is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

The student analyses low RÂ² values, comments on outliers and compares Pearson vs Spearman results, indicating thoughtful if not exhaustive critical analysis of methods and results.  The discussion is sound but lacks rigorous statistical significance testing or confidence intervals that would mark the highest band.

Limitations are identified, and future improvements (larger sample, non-linear models) are suggested, showing a clear evaluation of outcomes and their implications.  While comprehensive follow-up planning is limited, the studentâs evaluation exceeds âlimitedâ and satisfies the descriptor for band 2.

The reflection section recognises sample-size limits, impact of puberty, and other variables beyond age, showing meaningful engagement with findings.  The commentary is present throughout, but it stops short of deep critical insight or linking to broader literature; hence âmeaningful reflectionâ fits best.

Key symbols (Î£, r, r_s, \overline{T}, m, b) are used and definitions are given, but notation is sometimes inaccurate (e.g. 8.52.84 instead of 8 Ã 52.84; intercept written as 11.334 seconds; misuse of commas for decimals).  Mathematical language is understandable yet not consistently correct, fitting âsome appropriate useâ.

The investigation is clearly motivated by the studentâs personal swimming experience; the rationale, data collection and reflections are framed in the first-person context.  This provides clear evidence of a personal approach, though the approach is not especially innovative.

Most of the writing is relevant to the research question, yet lengthy narrative about personal swimming experience, repeated explanations of formulae and extensive reproduction of raw tables create noticeable redundancy.  The exploration could have conveyed the same mathematics more briefly, so it is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than fully concise.

Pearson, Spearman and linear regression are all relevant to the stated aim and are applied in a logical sequence.  The mathematics is appropriate and generally executed, so relevance is clear; however, the work does not extend beyond standard SL techniques to warrant higher bands.

Collecting primary stopwatch data and deciding to test separate correlation measures shows notable initiative beyond textbook examples, evidencing independent thinking.  Still, the analytical path (Pearson, Spearman, simple regression) remains fairly standard and does not push into âoutstandingâ territory.

Headings, sub-headings, a contents table and numbered sections make structure easy to navigate.  Figures and tables are placed near the discussion that refers to them, and the student ends with a separate conclusion.  Minor weaknessesâe.g. duplicate table headings and the rationale merging into the introductionâprevent it from reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ standard, but the work is certainly well organised with a recognisable beginning, middle and end.

The statistical tools used are fully commensurate with AA SL expectations and are applied to real, student-generated data.  Because there is no content above SL level, and some formula handling mistakes persist, âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3) is the best fit.

annotations.comment: The Pearson formula is correctly shown in LaTeX but the term Î£xy should be defined explicitly before use.
Bullet items embed math inline in an uneven style; separate narrative text from symbols for clearer presentation.
The expression â8.52.84â should be written as $$8\times52.84$$ when computing $$\Sigma xy$$ to avoid ambiguity.
Intermediate steps in the denominatorâs square-root calculation are omitted; include full steps to enhance clarity.
Describing rââ0.28 as âweak negative correlationâ is correct; consider including a comment on statistical significance.
The boysâ Pearson steps are collapsed into âfollow same stepsâ; full calculations would demonstrate independent mastery.
Claiming ânon-significant t-valuesâ without any t-test calculation is unsupported; either remove or include t-test.
Introduce the Spearman formula after the rank table to maintain logical progression of analysis steps.
Table 3 contains an extraneous LaTeX key row of artifacts at the bottom; remove to avoid confusion.
The computed $$\Sigma d^2$$ sum should match the table values; reconcile any discrepancy before substitution.
The Spearman coefficient calculation correctly captures the stronger monotonic trend, reflecting thoughtful method choice.
Table of Spearman ranks for girls is duplicated later as Table 4; renumber or replace with the boysâ rank table.
Figure 5 and Figure 6 are insightful, but each needs axis labels and a legend to reinforce clarity and interpretation.
The title is clear and directly states the focus of the investigation, aiding reader orientation.
The research question is explicit but could be more concise; consider tightening phrasing to reduce verbosity.
The table of contents provides a useful roadmap but shows inconsistent indentation and duplicate entries; streamline for clarity.
The introduction draws on personal experience vividly, showing authentic motivation and personal engagement.
The rationale paragraph ends abruptly without continuation; ensure complete exposition before subsection headings.
Personal narrative is engaging but verbose; consider focusing the introduction more tightly on mathematical rationale.
The aim statement is explicit in outlining methods but contains a fragmented sentence; merge or rephrase for coherence.
The aim clearly links to Pearsonâs correlation and regression modeling, showing appropriate mathematical planning.
Age variable is defined neatly with LaTeX, enhancing mathematical communication and precision.
Time descriptions omit explicit units in formulas; add âsâ consistently to maintain clarity.
The mean formula $$\overline{T}=\frac{\sum T_i}{3}$$ is correctly presented, demonstrating sound mathematical communication.
Repeating the mean calculation process for every group creates redundancy; consider summarizing and referring to one worked example.
Table 1 data use colon notation (e.g. 00:49:63) instead of decimal seconds; convert to 49.63 s for consistency.
Captions appear above tables rather than below; reposition captions to conform with standard presentation practice.
Figure 1 is effective but lacks axis labels and units; add âAge (years)â and âTime (s)â for clarity.
Figure 2 on the following page similarly needs clearly labeled axes and units to support interpretation.
The âAnalysis and summaryâ section would benefit from breaking into subsections with clear subheadings to improve structure.
The boysâ Spearman râââ0.43 interpretation is accurate and comments on other influencing factors show reflective depth.
The word âmonoticâ is misspelled; replace with âmonotonicâ for precise mathematical language.
The bullet âN + 10â is incorrect; clarify that n=10 age groups rather than âN + 10.â
Intercept b was calculated as 63.186 yet the regression line shows +11.334; correct inconsistent intercept values.
RÂ²â0.44 is calculated but its practical implication on model fit is not discussed; reflect on what this means for predictions.
Present the boysâ regression equation in LaTeX as $$\overline{T}=-1.78x+63.128$$ to maintain consistency.
The evaluation section ends mid-sentence; complete the critical discussion of limitations before concluding.
The conclusion restates results at length; consider summarizing key takeaways in a concise paragraph.
The final reflection duplicates earlier evaluation points; integrate rather than repeat for stronger coherence.
References lack a consistent citation style for dates and URLs; apply a uniform format throughout.
